ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: High-quality digital certificate issued by West Sweden Chamber of Commerce. All fields clearly legible.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: High-quality digital pro forma invoice. All fields clearly legible across both pages.

lc_check.lc_verification_reasoning: No LC reference found in the certificate. The remarks field contains '7200224861' which is a shipment/order reference number. The payment terms on page 2 of the invoice mention 'letter of credit' as an optional future payment method but no specific LC number is referenced in the certificate. LC exception not activated.

locations_found: Invoice page 2: footer 'Ericsson AB' (multiple occurrences in address/VAT/registration blocks)

criteria_met: Consignor 'ERICSSON AB' identified in invoice header, manufacturer line, and supplier line
Consignee 'KCELL JSC' identified in invoice delivery address
Shipment reference 7200224861 in certificate remarks closely matches shipment nr EAB-7200224860 in invoice (off by 1 digit — different references but same parties and goods confirm pairing)
pairing_validation.reasoning: At least two of three pairing criteria are clearly met: consignor ERICSSON AB is identified in the invoice, and consignee KCELL JSC is identified in the invoice delivery address. Document pairing is valid.

consignor.invoice_value: ERICSSON AB, Torshamnsgatan 21-23, S-164 83 STOCKHOLM, SWEDEN

comparison_steps.description: Search for certificate consignor name 'ERICSSON AB' anywhere in invoice
comparison_steps.result: FOUND - 'ERICSSON AB' found in invoice header (page 1), manufacturer line (page 1), and company details footer (page 2). Corporate structure rule gives MATCH.

comparison_steps.description: Compare consignor company name
comparison_steps.result: Certificate: 'ERICSSON AB' — Invoice header: 'ERICSSON AB'. Exact match after normalization.

comparison_steps.description: Verify consignor country SWEDEN
comparison_steps.result: Invoice header address explicitly states 'SWEDEN'. Additionally, VAT No SE556056625801 has SE prefix confirming Sweden. Country verified.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate country: SWEDEN. Invoice country: SWEDEN.
comparison_steps.result: Exact match. MATCH.

rules_applied.description: Corporate structure - consignor name found in invoice header and multiple locations
Country verified via explicit address and VAT prefix

consignor.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor is 'ERICSSON AB, TORSHAMNSGATAN 23, 164 80, STOCKHOLM, SWEDEN'. Invoice issuer is 'ERICSSON AB, Torshamnsgatan 21-23, S-164 83 STOCKHOLM, SWEDEN'. Company name 'ERICSSON AB' is an exact match. Country 'SWEDEN' matches exactly. Address shows minor variation (Torshamnsgatan 23 vs 21-23, postal code 164 80 vs 164 83) but per rule 4.1.5, differences in street address or postal code do not constitute MISMATCH for consignor — the decisive factors are company name and country. Both match perfectly.
consignor.motivation: Consignor 'ERICSSON AB' in certificate matches invoice issuer 'ERICSSON AB' exactly. Country SWEDEN confirmed both in explicit address and via VAT prefix SE. MATCH per rules 4.1.0, 4.1.2, 4.1.4.

consignee.certificate_value: KCELL JSC, ALIMZHANOVA STREET 51, 050004 ALMATY, KAZAKHSTAN
consignee.invoice_value: KCELL JSC, ALIMZHANOVA STREET 51, 050004, ALMATY, KAZAKHSTAN

comparison_steps.description: Check for primary billing fields in invoice (Invoice to, Bill to, Sold to, Buyer, To)
comparison_steps.result: Invoice does NOT contain a primary billing field (no 'Invoice to', 'Bill to', 'Sold to', 'Buyer' or 'To' field). The invoice is a Pro Forma Invoice with only a 'DELIVERY ADDRESS' block naming 'KCELL JSC'. Per rule 4.2.0.2 priority 3, when no primary billing field exists, the system may use delivery address as fallback.

comparison_steps.description: Since no primary billing field exists, search in secondary fields
comparison_steps.result: KCELL JSC found in DELIVERY ADDRESS block. This is acceptable per 4.2.1.1 since no primary billing field exists.

comparison_steps.description: Compare consignee company name
comparison_steps.result: Certificate: 'KCELL JSC' — Invoice: 'KCELL JSC'. Exact match.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate consignee country: KAZAKHSTAN. Invoice delivery country: KAZAKHSTAN.
comparison_steps.result: Exact match. MATCH.

rules_applied.description: Priority order - no primary billing field, delivery address used as fallback
Alternative recipient address - KCELL JSC found in delivery address
Name verification - exact match KCELL JSC

consignee.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee is 'KCELL JSC, ALIMZHANOVA STREET 51, 050004 ALMATY, KAZAKHSTAN'. The invoice is a Pro Forma Invoice and does not contain a primary billing field (Invoice to/Bill to/Sold to). The Delivery Address block lists 'KCELL JSC, ALIMZHANOVA STREET 51, 050004, ALMATY, KAZAKHSTAN'. Per rule 4.2.0.2 priority 3, when no primary billing field exists, the delivery address is an acceptable fallback. Company name 'KCELL JSC' matches exactly. Address matches exactly (same street, postal code, city). Country KAZAKHSTAN matches exactly. The 'Customer contact: NATALIYA BIEVA' further confirms KCELL as the customer party.
consignee.motivation: Consignee 'KCELL JSC' in certificate matches 'KCELL JSC' in invoice delivery address exactly. No primary billing field exists in invoice, so delivery address is used as fallback per 4.2.0.2. Country KAZAKHSTAN matches. Full address matches. MATCH.

goods_description.certificate_value:
42 PACKAGES
TELECOM EQUIPMENT
PL - AIR 3258 B78V and HWACs
PL - AIR 3258 B78V and HWACs
goods_description.invoice_value: Telecommunication Equipment: / AIR 3258 B78V and HWACs (two item lines)

comparison_steps.description: Check if certificate contains article numbers
comparison_steps.result: Certificate does not contain explicit article numbers (item/part numbers). The text 'AIR 3258 B78V' is a product model designation, not an article/part number. Proceed to Priority 2.

comparison_steps.description: Identify the identity-bearing main designation in the certificate
comparison_steps.result: Identity-bearing main designation: 'AIR 3258 B78V and HWACs'. Secondary designation: 'TELECOM EQUIPMENT'.

comparison_steps.description: Search for 'AIR 3258 B78V and HWACs' in invoice
comparison_steps.result: FOUND — Invoice contains 'AIR 3258 B78V and HWACs' in both item lines (2.1.1.1.1.1.1 and 2.1.1.2.1.1.1). Exact match after normalization.

comparison_steps.description: Search for 'TELECOM EQUIPMENT' in invoice
comparison_steps.result: Invoice header shows 'Telecommunication Equipment'. Certificate says 'TELECOM EQUIPMENT'. 'TELECOM' is a well-established abbreviation of 'Telecommunication'. The core identity-bearing designation 'AIR 3258 B78V and HWACs' already matches exactly, and the general category description 'TELECOM EQUIPMENT' vs 'Telecommunication Equipment' is a minor variation in the category-level descriptor. The primary product identification is via the model designation which matches exactly.

comparison_steps.description: Check if differences in descriptors affect product identity
comparison_steps.result: The prefix 'PL -' before 'AIR 3258 B78V and HWACs' in certificate denotes country of origin code, which is a non-identity-bearing addition per 4.3.4. The '42 PACKAGES' is a packaging/quantity notation. Neither changes product identity.

rules_applied.description: Controlled text matching - identity-bearing main designation 'AIR 3258 B78V and HWACs' matched exactly
Non-identity-bearing additions - 'PL -' prefix and '42 PACKAGES' do not affect product identity

goods_description.debug_explanation: Certificate goods description lists 'TELECOM EQUIPMENT' as category and 'AIR 3258 B78V and HWACs' as the specific product model (listed twice, presumably for two delivery locations). The invoice lists 'Telecommunication Equipment:' as category header and 'AIR 3258 B78V and HWACs' as the specific product description for both item lines. The identity-bearing main designation 'AIR 3258 B78V and HWACs' is found EXACTLY in the invoice (both lines). The category-level text 'TELECOM' vs 'Telecommunication' is a well-known abbreviation variation in the general category descriptor; the primary product identification is via the exact model designation. The prefix 'PL -' in the certificate denotes country of origin and is not identity-bearing per 4.3.4.
goods_description.motivation: The identity-bearing main designation 'AIR 3258 B78V and HWACs' in the certificate matches exactly with the invoice product descriptions. The general category 'TELECOM EQUIPMENT' corresponds to 'Telecommunication Equipment' in the invoice. MATCH.

quantity.invoice_value: Grand Total Gross Weight: 5 298,000 (page 1) / Gross Weight: 5.298,000 kg (page 2)

comparison_steps.description: Box 7 contains 'G.W 5218,000 KG'. This is the verification-grounding quantity.
comparison_steps.result: Verification-grounding quantity: GW 5218.000 KG from box 7.

comparison_steps.description: Search for numeric value 5218 or 5218.000 in invoice
comparison_steps.result: NOT FOUND. Invoice grand total gross weight is 5298.000 (page 1: '5 298,000'; page 2: '5.298,000 kg'). The value 5218 does not appear anywhere in the invoice.

comparison_steps.description: Normalize invoice values: '5 298,000' — the space is a thousands separator, comma separates decimals → 5298.000. '5.298,000' — the period is a thousands separator, comma separates decimals → 5298.000 kg.
comparison_steps.result: Invoice gross weight after normalization: 5298.000 kg. Certificate value: 5218.000 kg. These are different values.

comparison_steps.description: Check if the difference is within ±0.1% tolerance
comparison_steps.result: Difference: |5298.000 - 5218.000| = 80.000 kg. Tolerance at ±0.1% of 5218.000 = ±5.218 kg. Actual difference 80 kg far exceeds tolerance. MISMATCH.

comparison_steps.description: Check if summation of individual line gross weights matches certificate
comparison_steps.result: Invoice line 1 GW: 2232.000, line 2 GW: 3066.000. Sum: 2232.000 + 3066.000 = 5298.000. This equals the invoice grand total (5298.000) but NOT the certificate value (5218.000). Difference remains 80 kg. MISMATCH confirmed.

comparison_steps.description: Both documents explicitly state gross weight values that differ
comparison_steps.result: Certificate GW: 5218.000 KG. Invoice GW: 5298.000 kg. Both are explicit gross weight values. They differ by 80 kg (1.53%). This constitutes an explicit contradiction per 4.4.3.1.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate also states '42 PACKAGES' in box 6. Invoice states '42' in BOX column grand total and 'Box: 42' on page 2.
comparison_steps.result: 42 packages matches. However, per the critical rule in 4.4.3.1, an explicit contradiction in GW constitutes MISMATCH regardless of whether another quantity matches. The rule states: 'Att NW matchar räddar INTE en GW-motsägelse' and the same logic applies — a matching package count does not override a GW contradiction.

rules_applied.description: Box 7 value (GW 5218.000 KG) is the verification-grounding quantity
Direct occurrence check — value 5218 not found in invoice
Thousands separator normalization applied to invoice values
Explicit contradiction — certificate GW 5218.000 kg vs invoice GW 5298.000 kg, difference 80 kg exceeds ±0.1% tolerance

quantity.debug_explanation: Certificate box 7 states 'G.W 5218,000 KG' which normalizes to Gross Weight 5218.000 kg. The invoice states grand total gross weight as '5 298,000' on page 1 (with space as thousands separator = 5298.000) and 'Gross Weight: 5.298,000 kg' on page 2 (with period as thousands separator = 5298.000 kg). The certificate value is 5218.000 kg while the invoice value is 5298.000 kg — a difference of 80 kg (1.53%). This exceeds the ±0.1% tolerance (which would be ±5.218 kg). Both documents explicitly state gross weight values, creating an explicit contradiction. The certificate also states '42 PACKAGES' which matches the invoice's 42 boxes, but per rule 4.4.3.1 an explicit contradiction in GW cannot be overridden by a matching package count. The rule is clear: when both documents explicitly state a value for the same quantity type (GW) and these values differ beyond tolerance, MISMATCH. No exception (3A–3E) can resolve a fundamental numeric discrepancy.
quantity.motivation: Certificate states G.W. 5218,000 KG. Invoice states Gross Weight 5.298,000 kg (= 5298.000 kg). Difference is 80 kg (1.53%), which exceeds the ±0.1% tolerance. This is an explicit contradiction in gross weight. MISMATCH per rule 4.4.3.1.

country_of_origin.invoice_value: PL (COUNTRY OF ORIGIN column, both item lines)

comparison_steps.description: Search for origin indication in invoice
comparison_steps.result: Invoice has a dedicated 'COUNTRY OF ORIGIN' column. Both item lines show 'PL'. Page 2 states 'Country of Origin: mentioned above' referring back to the per-item column.

comparison_steps.description: Compare 'PL' (certificate) with 'PL' (invoice)
comparison_steps.result: Exact match. PL = Poland (ISO alpha-2 code).

comparison_steps.description: Certificate header indicates 'EUROPEAN UNION' certificate type. Box 3 origin is 'PL'. Poland is an EU member state.
comparison_steps.result: PL (Poland) is an EU-27 member state. The EU certificate header is consistent with a Polish origin. No conflict.

rules_applied.description: Origin identified in dedicated COUNTRY OF ORIGIN column in invoice
ISO code normalization — PL matches exactly

country_of_origin.debug_explanation: Certificate box 3 states origin as 'PL' (Poland). The invoice has a dedicated 'COUNTRY OF ORIGIN' column where both item lines explicitly state 'PL'. This is an exact match. Poland is an EU member state, consistent with the EU Certificate of Origin format. Page 2 of the invoice also notes 'Country of Origin: mentioned above' reinforcing that origin is specified per item. No ambiguity or contradiction.
country_of_origin.motivation: Certificate origin 'PL' matches invoice COUNTRY OF ORIGIN column 'PL' exactly on both item lines. Poland is confirmed as an EU member state. MATCH.

rule_evaluation_log.reasoning: No LC reference found in certificate. The reference '7200224861' in box 5 is a shipment/order reference, not an LC number. No bank-term context found.
Two files received. Document A is identified as Certificate of Origin (EU/ICC). Document B is identified as Pro Forma Invoice. No embedded invoices in certificate file. Standard pairing applied.
Consignor ERICSSON AB found in invoice. Consignee KCELL JSC found in invoice delivery address. Pairing validated.
ERICSSON AB found in invoice header, manufacturer line, supplier line, and footer company details.
ERICSSON AB matches exactly.
SWEDEN matches SWEDEN.
No primary billing field found. Delivery address used as fallback (priority 3).
KCELL JSC matches exactly.
KAZAKHSTAN matches KAZAKHSTAN.
Identity-bearing main designation 'AIR 3258 B78V and HWACs' found exactly in invoice.
Box 7 contains GW 5218.000 KG. This is the primary verification quantity.
5218.000 not found in invoice. Invoice shows 5298.000 kg GW.
Only one invoice present.
Both values already in KG. No unit conversion needed.
Certificate does not state per-unit weight.
Sum of line GW: 2232.000 + 3066.000 = 5298.000. This matches invoice grand total but NOT certificate value (5218.000). Difference remains.

rule_evaluation_log.rule_description: Exception 3E — summation of line weights when total row missing
rule_evaluation_log.reasoning: Invoice has a total row. 3E is for when total row is missing.
Certificate GW 5218.000 kg vs Invoice GW 5298.000 kg. Both explicit. Difference 80 kg (1.53%) exceeds ±0.1% tolerance. Explicit contradiction.
PL found in dedicated COUNTRY OF ORIGIN column.
PL matches PL exactly.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The value 5218 in the certificate could be a thousands-separator misread of 5.218 (= 5218)
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: The certificate clearly states '5218,000 KG' where the comma is the decimal separator. The value is unambiguously 5218.000 kg. The invoice states 5298.000 kg. These are different numbers.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The 80 kg difference could be within acceptable tolerance
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Per rule 4.4.5.2.4, the only allowed tolerance is ±0.1% or ±0.001 of the stated unit. 0.1% of 5218 = 5.218 kg. The actual difference is 80 kg, which is approximately 15x the allowed tolerance. Per rule 4.4.3.1 (Förbud mot uppfunna toleranser), no invented tolerance may be applied.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The 42 packages matching could override the GW mismatch
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Per rule 4.4.3.1, an explicit contradiction in GW cannot be overridden by a matching count in another quantity type. The rule explicitly states that matching one quantity does not 'save' a contradiction in another when both documents explicitly state values for the same type.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Certificate reference 7200224861 might indicate an LC
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Per rule 2.2 (KRITISK REGEL – Krav på banktermskontext), the LC exception requires an explicit bank-term label (L/C, Letter of Credit, etc.). The reference '7200224861' is a shipment/order number without any bank-term context. LC exception not activated.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Certifikatet anger bruttovikt 5 218,000 kg medan fakturan anger bruttovikt 5 298,000 kg — en skillnad på 80 kg. Avsändare, mottagare, varubeskrivning och ursprungsland överensstämmer, men viktavvikelsen innebär att dokumenten inte kan godkännas automatiskt.
overall_assessment.summary: Four of five checkpoints MATCH (consignor, consignee, goods description, country of origin). Quantity shows MISMATCH: certificate states GW 5218.000 KG while invoice states GW 5298.000 kg — a difference of 80 kg (1.53%) exceeding the ±0.1% tolerance. This constitutes an explicit contradiction in gross weight.
blocking_issues: Quantity MISMATCH: Certificate GW 5218.000 KG vs Invoice GW 5298.000 kg (difference 80 kg, 1.53%)

sections.invoice_value: ERICSSON AB, Torshamnsgatan 21-23, S-164 83 STOCKHOLM, SWEDEN
sections.rule_applied: Företagsnamn exakt identiskt. Land SWEDEN verifierat via adress och VAT-prefix SE.
sections.motivation: ERICSSON AB matchar exakt mellan certifikat och faktura. Land SWEDEN bekräftat. Smärre adressvariationer (gatunummer 23 vs 21-23, postnummer 164 80 vs 164 83) påverkar inte bedömningen.

sections.certificate_value: KCELL JSC, ALIMZHANOVA STREET 51, 050004 ALMATY, KAZAKHSTAN
sections.invoice_value: KCELL JSC, ALIMZHANOVA STREET 51, 050004, ALMATY, KAZAKHSTAN (Delivery Address)
sections.rule_applied: Fakturan saknar primärt faktureringsfält. Leveransadress används som fallback. Exakt namnmatchning.
sections.motivation: KCELL JSC matchar exakt. Adress och land KAZAKHSTAN matchar. Inget primärt faktureringsfält finns, så leveransadressen används som fallback.

sections.certificate_value: 42 PACKAGES TELECOM EQUIPMENT PL - AIR 3258 B78V and HWACs
sections.invoice_value: Telecommunication Equipment: AIR 3258 B78V and HWACs (two item lines)
sections.rule_applied: Identitetsbärande huvudbeteckning 'AIR 3258 B78V and HWACs' matchad exakt via kontrollerad textmatchning.
sections.motivation: Produktbeteckningen 'AIR 3258 B78V and HWACs' förekommer exakt i båda dokumenten. Övriga tillägg (42 PACKAGES, PL-prefix, 'Telecom' vs 'Telecommunication') är icke-identitetsbärande.

sections.invoice_value: Gross Weight: 5.298,000 kg / Grand Total GW: 5 298,000
sections.rule_applied: Direkt förekomstkontroll. Explicita bruttovikter i båda dokumenten skiljer sig med 80 kg — överstiger ±0,1% tolerans.
sections.motivation: Certifikatet anger bruttovikt 5 218,000 kg. Fakturan anger bruttovikt 5 298,000 kg. Skillnaden är 80 kg (1,53%), vilket överstiger den tillåtna toleransen på ±0,1% (5,218 kg). Båda dokumenten anger uttryckligen bruttovikt, vilket utgör en explicit motsägelse.

sections.rule_applied: Exakt matchning av ISO-landskod PL (Polen) i dedikerad ursprungskolumn.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets ursprungsland PL matchar exakt med fakturans COUNTRY OF ORIGIN-kolumn som anger PL för båda artikelraderna. Polen är EU-medlemsstat, konsistent med EU-certifikatet.

output_quality_checks.consistency_check_details: One checkpoint (quantity) has MISMATCH status. comparison_result is NOT_IDENTICAL. workflow_recommendation is MANUAL_HANDLING_REQUIRED. All consistent.
